title: padus avium var. glauca
intro: Padus avium var. glauca, also known as the silver-leaved dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its distinctive silver-gray foliage. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Padus avium var. glauca have been found to provide relief from various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as offering antioxidant and antimicrobial benefits. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to promote overall well-being and longevity.
